---
title: "GeodeticOps.jl"
engine: julia
---

```{julia}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
using GeodeticOps, CairoMakie
```

**GeodeticOps.jl** is a pure-Julia package for geodetic computations on Earth's ellipsoid.

## Features

- **7 coordinate types** with automatic conversions: LLA, LLARad, ECEF, ENU, NED, AER, UTM
- **Geodesic distances**: Haversine (great-circle) and Vincenty (ellipsoidal)
- **Forward and inverse geodesic problems** via Vincenty's formulae
- **Geodesic interpolation**: intermediate points and midpoints along geodesics
- **Polygon area** on the ellipsoid using spherical excess
- **Bounding boxes**: axis-aligned and distance-based
- **Multiple datums**: WGS84 (default), GRS80, Clarke 1866, WGS72

## Installation

```julia
using Pkg
Pkg.add("GeodeticOps")
```

## Quick Example

```{julia}
using GeodeticOps

nyc = LLA(-74.006, 40.7128)
london = LLA(-0.1278, 51.5074)

d = distance(nyc, london)
println("NYC -> London: $(round(d / 1000, digits=1)) km")
println("Bearing:       $(round(bearing(nyc, london), digits=1))deg")
println("Midpoint:      $(midpoint(nyc, london))")
```

All operations default to WGS84.  Pass `datum=GRS80` (or any `Datum`) to override, or set `DEFAULT_DATUM[] = GRS80` to change the global default.

```{julia}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-hero
#| fig-cap: "Geodesic path (blue) versus rhumb line (dashed gray) from New York City to London.  The geodesic curves northward because it follows the shortest path on the ellipsoid."
n = 100
geo = [intermediate_point(nyc, london, f) for f in range(0, 1, n)]
geo_lons = [p.lon for p in geo]
geo_lats = [p.lat for p in geo]

rhumb_lons = collect(range(nyc.lon, london.lon, n))
rhumb_lats = collect(range(nyc.lat, london.lat, n))

fig = Figure(size=(700, 350))
ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="Longitude (deg)", ylabel="Latitude (deg)")
lines!(ax, rhumb_lons, rhumb_lats, color=:gray60, linewidth=1.5, linestyle=:dash, label="Rhumb line")
lines!(ax, geo_lons, geo_lats, color=:steelblue, linewidth=2.5, label="Geodesic")
scatter!(ax, [nyc.lon, london.lon], [nyc.lat, london.lat], color=:crimson, markersize=12)
text!(ax, nyc.lon, nyc.lat - 1.8; text="NYC", fontsize=13, align=(:center, :top))
text!(ax, london.lon + 1.5, london.lat + 0.5; text="London", fontsize=13, align=(:left, :bottom))
axislegend(ax, position=:rb)
fig
```
